name: Nana Buluku
description: Nana Buluku is the supreme creator goddess in the mythology of the Fon people of Benin, Togo, and southwestern Nigeria, and is also revered in Yoruba traditions. She is considered the mother of Mawu and Lisa, the moon and sun deities respectively, who are often seen as twin creators of the visible world. After creating the universe and birthing the orishas, Nana Buluku retreated from direct involvement in the affairs of the world, becoming a distant and enigmatic figure. She is often associated with creation, motherhood, justice, and cosmic order. Nana Buluku's power lies in her primordial role as the source of all things, and she is seen as a wise and impartial judge. She is not typically directly worshipped in the same way as other orishas, but is honored as the ultimate origin and sustainer of existence. Her influence is felt through the actions and powers of her descendants, Mawu and Lisa, and her presence is a reminder of the deeper cosmic forces at play. Some sources state she is hermaphroditic or androgynous, representing the unity of male and female principles in creation. She represents the ultimate authority and the source from which all other deities and beings derive their power. Nana Buluku is a powerful and revered figure, embodying the mysteries of creation and the importance of balance and order in the universe.
subtitle: Supreme Creator Goddess of Yoruba Pantheon

keyMyths.title: The Creation of the Universe
keyMyths.description: Nana Buluku created the universe and then gave birth to Mawu and Lisa, who then created the world as we know it. After this creation, she withdrew from direct involvement.
keyMyths.source: Various oral traditions and scholarly interpretations

keyMyths.title: Birth of Mawu and Lisa
keyMyths.description: Mawu and Lisa are the twin deities of the moon and sun, respectively. Nana Buluku, as their mother, established them as the primary forces shaping the world.
keyMyths.source: Various oral traditions and scholarly interpretations

keyMyths.title: Nana Buluku's Retreat
keyMyths.description: After creating the world and establishing the roles of the orishas, Nana Buluku withdrew from direct intervention, becoming a distant but powerful force. This highlights her role as the ultimate source.
keyMyths.source: Various oral traditions and scholarly interpretations

sources.author: M.J. Herskovits
